summary: Crystal‑clear audience recording of a stellar Philly run with a smoking Help > Slip > Franklin’s sequence
review: The tape is repeatedly hailed as the best audience (AUD) capture in the Dead vault – crystal clear, deep bass and vivid sub‑sonics. Musically the show shines especially in the second set where the Help on the Way → Slipknot! → Franklin's Tower suite, Playing In The Band and China Doll are highlighted, though a few listeners note the third “I Don't Need Love” as a weak spot.

ai_rating.rationale: Show is consistently praised with only one moderate comment; recording quality is described as exceptional, yielding an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as crystal clear, with deep bass that “rises from beneath you,” superb sub‑sonics and noted as possibly the best AUD tape ever made.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: The second set is a highlight, especially the seamless jam after Slipknot leading into Franklin's Tower; overall pacing and transitions are praised.
